name: reply_to_conversation
description: Send a reply to an existing conversation in Intercom. Reply as an admin (support agent) to respond to customer inquiries, or as a user to simulate customer messages. Supports plain text and rich HTML content including links, bold, italic, and lists. Replying as an admin can optionally reopen a closed conversation. Each reply creates a new conversation part in the thread.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique conversation ID. This is a path parameter in the URL.

parameters.name: message_type
parameters.description: The type of message: 'comment' for a visible reply in the conversation thread, or 'note' for an internal note visible only to admins.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Who is sending the reply: 'admin' for a support agent or 'user' for a contact/customer.

parameters.name: admin_id
parameters.description: The ID of the admin sending the reply. Required when type is 'admin'.

parameters.name: intercom_user_id
parameters.description: The Intercom contact ID of the user sending the reply. Required when type is 'user'.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
The message body. Supports HTML for rich formatting:

, , , ,

    /
  • ,
    .

parameters.name: attachment_urls
parameters.description: Array of publicly accessible URLs for file attachments to include with the reply.

body.body:
Hi Jane,

Thanks for reaching out! I've looked into the billing page issue and our engineering team has deployed a fix. Could you try accessing the billing settings again?

If the issue persists, please try clearing your browser cache or using an incognito window.

Best,
Sarah

conversation_parts.body:
Hi Jane,

Thanks for reaching out! I've looked into the billing page issue and our engineering team has deployed a fix. Could you try accessing the billing settings again?

If the issue persists, please try clearing your browser cache or using an incognito window.

Best,
Sarah
